methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RMFG, a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Kenneth Cassel, has raised $4.5M to date. The relevant comp universe comprises 9 private robotics and manufacturing firms—including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity—alongside 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ). A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow robotics and manufacturing cohort; only 7 peers exist in the sector overall, none meeting the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints at similar valuation tiers.

The stage-progression method provides the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised ($4.5M) as a base proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $11.25M. This method is applicable given RMFG's explicit seed positioning and the established pattern of capital step-ups across venture stages.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a median valuation of $11M, with a plausible range of $10M (low) to $13M (high). Key uncertainties include the composition and stage distribution of peer robotics companies—none currently offering direct valuation benchmarking—and the absence of disclosed revenue or product-stage metrics. A peer-median benchmark would strengthen conviction once comparable seed or early Series A robotics firms with public data emerge.
